data.title: Strive Closes Semler Deal, Taps Avik Roy As Strategy Chief
data.description:
Strive, Inc. (NASDAQ:ASST) stock fell Friday following the company’s announcement of the successful completion of its Semler Scientific acquisition. The stock’s decline comes as broader markets are mixed, with the S&P 500 slightly up while the Dow Jones is down.

Strive now holds approximately 12,797.9 bitcoins, making it the 11th-largest publicly traded corporate holder of Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC/USD) globally. Additionally, Avik Roy has been appointed as Chief Strategy Officer, focusing on monetizing Semler Scientific’s operating business by expanding its mandate into early-disease detection products.

The company has also welcomed Eric Semler, former chairman of Semler Scientific, as an independent board member, and Joe Burnett, previously director of Bitcoin Strategy for Semler Scientific, has joined as VP of Bitcoin Strategy. This strategic move aims to enhance Strive’s position in the healthcare sector while leveraging its bitcoin holdings.

ASST Struggles with Long-Term Momentum

The stock is currently trading 5.2% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) but is 56.4% below its 100-day SMA, indicating a struggle to maintain momentum in the longer term. Over the past 12 months, shares have increased by 102.98% and are currently positioned closer to their 52-week lows than highs.

The RSI is at 50.75, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting no strong momentum in either direction. Meanwhile, MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum.

The combination of neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum for the stock.

  • Key Resistance: $1.00
  • Key Support: $0.50

Strive’s Earnings Expectation: EPS Growth Forecast

Investors are looking ahead to the company’s next earnings report on March 30, 2026, with analysts expecting earnings per share of $-0.01, up from $-0.21 from the same quarter last year, and revenue of $1.80 million, up from $0.21 million from the same quarter last year. Strive, Inc. Class A Common Stock has a consensus Buy rating among analysts with an average price target of $1.50.

Benzinga Edge Ranks Strive as ‘High-Flyer’

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Strive, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Momentum: Bullish (Score: 83/100) — Stock is outperforming the broader market.
  • Quality: Solid (Score: 66/100) — Balance sheet remains healthy.
  • Value: Risk (Score: 4/100) — Trading at a steep premium relative to peers.

The Verdict: Strive’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a classic ‘High-Flyer’ setup. While the Momentum (83) confirms the strong trend, the extremely low Value (4) score warns that the stock is priced for perfection—investors should ride the trend but use tight stop-losses.

ASST Price Action: Strive shares were down 0.26% at $0.96 at the time of publication on Friday. The stock is near its 52-week low of $0.38, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: TransDigm Drops $2.2 Billion To Bulk Up Its Aircraft Parts Empire
data.description:
TransDigm Group Incorporated (NYSE:TDG) on Friday said it signed a definitive agreement to buy Jet Parts Engineering and Victor Sierra Aviation Holdings from Vance Street Capital in a cash deal valued at about $2.2 billion, including certain tax benefits.

The two businesses focus on proprietary, FAA-approved aftermarket parts and repair solutions that offer airlines and aviation operators alternatives to OEM components.

Jet Parts Engineering (Seattle) designs OEM-alternative components and repairs largely for commercial airline and MRO customers, while Victor Sierra Aviation Holdings supplies proprietary parts and services mainly for business and general aviation through brands including McFarlane Aviation and Tempest Aero Group. Nearly all revenue at both companies comes from the commercial aftermarket.

Together, the companies generated about $280 million in 2025 revenue and employ roughly 700 people. JPE operates engineering and repair sites across the U.S. and the United Kingdom; Victor Sierra runs primary facilities in Kansas, North Carolina and Illinois with additional satellite locations.

CEO Mike Lisman said, “We are excited to have an agreement to acquire Jet Parts Engineering and Victor Sierra, two well-run, profitable businesses that will fit well within TransDigm.” Chairman Nick Howley called the move “a natural progression for TransDigm,” pointing to the company’s long-standing efforts in PMA and aftermarket engineering.

The acquisition follows a recent agreement to buy Stellant Systems for $960 million, reinforcing the company’s focus on specialized aerospace assets with recurring demand.

TransDigm held cash and cash equivalents of $2.808 billion as of September 30, 2025.

TDG Price Action: TransDigm Group shares were up 1.66% at $1457.34 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Shutterstock

data.title: Locafy Shares Surge After Expanding Partnership With Experience.com
data.description:
Locafy Limited (NASDAQ:LCFY) shares are trading higher Friday after the company announced it expanded its partnership with Experience.com.

Locafy stock is showing exceptional strength. Why are LCFY shares rallying?

Locafy, Experience.com Expand U.S. Partnership

Locafy said the expanded agreement allows Experience.com to sell Locafy's Localizer product across the United States alongside its reviews and reputation-management platform.

Under the expanded partnership, Experience.com will initially target more than 800,000 recently added home services business profiles on its platform, including contractors across roofing, plumbing, HVAC, and related trades.

Locafy said it will support Experience.com's sales efforts through educational webinars and AI-powered sales enablement technology designed to deliver qualified appointments directly to Experience.com's sales team.

Experience.com said it is targeting thousands of Localizer sales during 2026, subject to market conditions and execution, citing early traction and pipeline development.

Locafy said the partnership expansion is intended to scale adoption of its Localizer solution through a channel-led go-to-market strategy in the U.S. home services market.

The partnership will also be supported by Growth Pro Agency, Locafy's premium U.S. partner, which has sold and deployed Locafy's technology across the home services sector.

Technical Analysis Of Locafy

Locafy is trading 83.5% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 27.7% above its 100-day SMA, indicating strong short-term momentum. Over the past 12 months, shares have decreased by 30.09%, and the stock is currently positioned closer to its 52-week lows than highs, reflecting a challenging longer-term trend.

The RSI is at 40.68, which is considered neutral territory, suggesting that the stock is neither overbought nor oversold. Meanwhile, the MACD is above its signal line, indicating bullish momentum, which could support the current price increase.

The combination of a neutral RSI and bullish MACD suggests mixed momentum, indicating that while there is some upward pressure, caution may still be warranted.

LCFY Price Action: At the time of writing, Locafy shares are trading 48.56% higher at $4.65, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

This illustration was generated using artificial intelligence via Midjourney.
